findings.message: Database migration is missing the owner_email column. The ORM model in src/demo/db/models.py adds owner_email (line 17), but the Alembic migration does not include this column in the create_table statement. This will cause runtime errors when the application tries to access or store owner_email.
findings.suggestion: Create a new migration file that adds the owner_email column: ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN owner_email VARCHAR(254) NULL;

findings.message: The post_item function receives owner_email in the ItemCreate payload but does not pass it to create_item(). The repository function signature create_item(session, name) does not accept owner_email as a parameter, so any owner_email provided by the client is silently discarded.
findings.suggestion: Either (1) update create_item() to accept and persist owner_email, or (2) document that owner_email should come from an authenticated user context, not from the request payload. If owner_email should not be stored, remove it from ItemCreate schema to avoid user confusion.
